item.title: Phoenix HTML 函数组件注意事项 | Notes on HTML function Components with Phoenix
item.description:
一些可以在表单中使用的未经修饰的简单 HTML功能组件。

表单组件

我们构建了一个带有表单的:live_component来使用这些 HTML 组件。 我们使用无模式变更集MyForm来验证数据。 它基于 struct %MyForm{}导出函数changeset/2 。

如果需要，我们在mount/1中实例化分配，即如果不存在。 这些是将发送到数据库的字段。 他们将给出初始默认值。

 def mount ( socket ) do { :ok , assign ( socket , changeset: MyForm . changeset (% MyForm {}), [ ... other assigns ... ] )} end

我们声明将读取的分配以捕获render/1中的差异：

 def render ( assigns ) do assigns = assign ( assigns , .... [ ... declare the accessed fields ... ] ~H"" " <div> <.form for={@changeset} id=" my_form " phx-change=" change " phx-submit=" send " phx-target={@myself} class=" ... " > [...HTML function components here...] </.form>

所有 formData 字段都将使用“my_form”的ID值进行命名空间，例如name="my_form[distance]" 。 我们在“change”上添加事件处理程序来解析表单的参数，检查约束并使用changeset和assigns上的更改更新套接字。

 def handle_event ( "change" , %{ "my_form" => params }, socket ) do changeset = % MyForm {} |> MyForm . changeset ( params ) |> Map . put ( :action , :validate ) socket = socket |> assign ( :changeset , changeset ) [ ... other assigns ... ] { :noreply , socket } end

我们使用编译时验证（而不是运行时）声明组件属性。

 slot ( :inner_block ) attr ( :some_attr , :string )

注意：设置required: true到slot意味着您不能使用自闭合组件。

施工过程

  • 声明一个函数并使用“attributes={@assign}”。 例如：
 <. html_function attrib1 = { @assign1 } attr2 = { @assign2 } > <. inner_code /> </. html_function

  • 声明用于编译时检查的属性：
    attr(:name, :string)

  • 检查mount/1或render/1中的分配

  • 构建函数组件以使用属性呈现 HTML 元素

def html_function ( assigns ) do ~H"" " <label for={@attrib1}> <input id={@attrib1} value={attrib2} name=.../> </label> ... """ end
  • 如果使用带有变更集的“更改”，则使用更改的值更新套接字分配

传递隐藏输入的示例

我们想将给定的值传递给 formData，但没有明确的输入。 您可以使用hidden属性。

使用“纯 HTML”显然非常容易，如下所示：

 <input type= "hidden" name= "my_form[radius]" value= {@distance} />

但是，该过程是用一个功能 HTML 组件来说明的。

  • 用法：使用带有属性的函数说pass_assing ，该属性使用我们要传递的分配：
 <. pass_assign radius = { @distance } />

  • 分配可能已经存在于分配中，

  • 属性声明：

 attr ( :radius , :float , doc: false )
 def pass_assign ( assigns ) do ~H"" " <input type=" hidden " value={@radius} name=" my_form [ radius ] " /> """ end
  • 该值没有更改处理程序，表单将提交：
 %{"my_form" => %{"radius" => 12345}}

可重复使用的日期输入示例

当我们想要重用date类型的输入时，一个更有趣的例子。

 <. date date = { @start_date } name = "start" > </. date > <. date date = { @end_date } name = "end" > </. date >

您有两个属性和两个分配。

  • 在 live_component mount/1中，实例化使用的分配：
 # def mount(socket) { :ok , assign ( socket , ... start_date: Date . utc_today (), end_date: Date . utc_today () |> Date . add ( 1 ), name: "" }
  • 声明组件中使用的属性
attr ( :name , :string ) attr ( :date , :string )
  • 构建函数date/1并使用属性：
 def date ( assigns ) do ~H"" " <input type=" date " id={@name} name={" myform [ #{@name}_date]"} value = { @date } class = "w-60" /> """ end
  • 在render(assigns)中，声明访问的分配：
 assigns = assign ( assigns , :start_date , assigns . start_date ) assigns = assign ( assigns , :end_date , assigns . end_date )
  • 更新“更改”事件处理程序中的分配：
 socket = socket |> assign ( :start_date , params [ "start_date" ]) |> assign ( :end_date , params [ "end_date" ])

单个选择菜单示例

一个更好的例子：我们可以在单个选择功能组件中轻松地从菜单中捕获所选值。 我们使用selected的布尔 HTML 属性来捕获从菜单中选择的“选项”以传递给 formData。

注意：如果是多选的情况，即使我们使用一个列表而不是单个字符串，我们也需要管理这个列表中可能发生的变化。

  • 用法：我们有两个输入——菜单列表和初始选择——和两个属性。
 <. select options = { @menu } select = { @choice } />
  • 我们在mount/1中实例化分配：
 # mount(socket) { :ok , assign ( socket , changeset: MyForm . changeset (% MyForm {}), menu: [ "a" , "b" , "c" ], choice: "" , [ ... ] }
  • 我们声明了两个组件属性：
 attr ( :options , :string ) attr ( :select , :string
  • 我们构建功能 HTML 组件select/1
 def select ( assigns ) do ~H"" " <select id=" select " name=" my_form [ select ] "> <option :for={option <- @options} selected={option == @select} > <%= option %> </option> </select> """ end

并提交（在“变更集”验证之后）带有事件处理程序“变更”的新值到分配中：

 socket = socket |> assign ( :changeset , changeset ) |> assign ( :choice , params [ "select" ])

动态数据列表示例

动态填充datalist的示例。
我们使用关联的input将值传递给 formData。

我们想要一个在用户开始输入时填充的数据列表。

  • 用法：datalist组件可以是：
 <. datalist users = { @users } user = { @user } />

  • 分配：我们在mount/1中实例化两个分配users: []和user: "" ，
    或根据需要/需要在render/1中设置user: assign.current_user 。

  • 功能组件是：

 def datalist ( assigns ) do ~H"" " <input list=" datalist " id=" datalist - input " name=" my_form [ user ] " phx-change=" search - email " value={@user} /> <datalist id=" datalist "> <option :for={user <- @users} value={user} /> </datalist> """ end

并且“更改”处理程序可以像：

 def handle_event ( "search-email" , %{ "my_form" => %{ "user" => string }}, socket ) do datalist = MyApp . User . search ( string ) |> Enum . map ( & &1 . email ) { :noreply , assign ( socket , users: datalist , user: string } end

一旦暂停，ndrean 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，ndrean 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，ndrean 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 ndrean 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 ndrean：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 ndrean 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

A few unpolished simple HTML functional components that can be used in a form.

The form component

We build a :live_component with a form to use these HTML components. We use a schemaless changeset MyForm to validate the data. It exports a function changeset/2 based on a struct %MyForm{}.

We instantiate the assigns in mount/1 if needed, ie if not already existing. These are the fields that will be sent to be database. They will give the initial default values.

def mount(socket) do {:ok, assign(socket, changeset: MyForm.changeset(%MyForm{}), [... other assigns...] )}
end

We declare the assigns that will be read to capture diffs in the render/1:

def render(assigns) do assigns = assign(assigns, .... [...declare the accessed fields...] ~H""" <div> <.form for={@changeset} id="my_form" phx-change="change" phx-submit="send" phx-target={@myself} class="..." > [...HTML function components here...] </.form>

All the formData fields will be name-spaced with the ID value of "my_form", such as name="my_form[distance]". The "send" handler will receive a formData of the form:

%{"my_form" => %{"val1" => "a", "val2" => 2,...}}

We add the event handler on "change" to parse the form's params, check for the constraints and update the socket with the changes on the changeset and assigns.

def handle_event("change", %{"my_form" => params}, socket) do changeset = %MyForm{} |> MyForm.changeset(params) |> Map.put(:action, :validate) socket = socket |> assign(:changeset, changeset) [... other assigns...] {:noreply, socket}
end

We declare the component attributes with the compile-time validation (not runtime).

slot(:inner_block)
attr(:some_attr, :string)

Note: setting required: true to the slot means you can't use a self-closing component.

Construction process

  • declare a function and use "attributes={@assign}". For example: 
<.html_function attrib1={@assign1} attr2={@assign2}> <.inner_code />
</.html_function

  • declare the attributes used for compile-time checks:
    attr(:name, :string)

  • check for the assigns in mount/1 or render/1

  • build the function component to render an HTML element using the attributes

def html_function(assigns) do ~H""" <label for={@attrib1}> <input id={@attrib1} value={attrib2} name=.../> </label> ... """
end
  • if a "change" with a changeset is used, update the socket assigns with the changed values

Example of passing a hidden input

We want to pass a given value into the formData but without an explicit input. You can use the hidden attribute.

It is clearly very easy to just use "plain HTML" as below:

<input type="hidden" name="my_form[radius]" value={@distance} />

However, the process is illustrate with a function HTML component.

  • usage: use a function say pass_assing with an attribute that uses the assign we want to pass: 
<.pass_assign radius={@distance} />

  • the assign is probably already present in the assigns,

  • the attribute is declared:

attr(:radius, :float, doc: false)

def pass_assign(assigns) do ~H""" <input type="hidden" value={@radius} name="my_form[radius]" /> """
end
  • there is no change handler for this value, and the form will be submitted with: 
%{"my_form" => %{"radius" => 12345}}

Example of a reusable date input

An example a bit more interesting when we want to reuse an input of type date.

 <.date date={@start_date} name="start"></.date> <.date date={@end_date} name="end"></.date>

You have two attributes and two assigns.

  • in the live_component mount/1, instantiate the assigns used: 
# def mount(socket)
{:ok, assign(socket, ... start_date: Date.utc_today(), end_date: Date.utc_today() |> Date.add(1), name: ""
}
  • declare the attributes used in the component 
attr(:name, :string)
attr(:date, :string)
  • build the function date/1 and use the attributes: 
def date(assigns) do ~H""" <input type="date" id={@name} name={"myform[#{@name}_date]"} value={@date} class="w-60" /> """
end
  • in the render(assigns), declare the accessed assigns: 
assigns = assign(assigns, :start_date, assigns.start_date)
assigns = assign(assigns, :end_date, assigns.end_date)
  • update the assigns in the "change" event handler: 
socket = socket |> assign(:start_date, params["start_date"]) |> assign(:end_date, params["end_date"])

Example of a single Select menu

A better example: we can easily capture the selected value from the menu in a single select functional component. We use the boolean HTML attribute selected to capture the selected "option" from the menu to pass to the formData.

note: if the case of multiple selects, even if we use a list instead of a single string, we need to manage the possible changes in this list.

  • usage: we have two inputs - the menu list and the initial choice - and two attributes. 
<.select options={@menu} select={@choice}/>
  • we instantiate the assigns in the mount/1: 
# mount(socket)
{:ok, assign(socket, changeset: MyForm.changeset(%MyForm{}), menu: ["a", "b", "c"], choice: "", [...]
}
  • we declare the two component attributes: 
attr(:options, :string)
attr(:select, :string
  • we build the function HTML component select/1 
def select(assigns) do ~H""" <select id="select" name="my_form[select]"> <option :for={option <- @options} selected={option == @select} > <%= option %> </option> </select> """
end

and commit (after the "changeset" validation) the new value with the event handler "change" into the assigns:

socket = socket |> assign(:changeset, changeset) |> assign(:choice, params["select"])

Example of a dynamic Datalist

An example with a dynamically populated datalist.
We use the associated input to pass the value to the formData.

We want a datalist that gets populated when the user starts typing.

  • usage: the datalist component can be: 
<.datalist users={@users} user={@user}/>

  • assigns: we instantiate the two assigns users: [] and user: "" in mount/1,
    or set user: assign.current_user in render/1 if needed/desired.

  • attributes: we declare the two attributes

attr(:users, :list)
attr(:user, :string)
  • the function component is: 
def datalist(assigns) do ~H""" <input list="datalist" id="datalist-input" name="my_form[user]" phx-change="search-email" value={@user} /> <datalist id="datalist"> <option :for={user <- @users} value={user} /> </datalist> """
end

and the "change" handler can be like:

def handle_event("search-email", %{"my_form" => %{"user" => string}}, socket) do datalist = MyApp.User.search(string) |> Enum.map(& &1.email) {:noreply, assign(socket, users: datalist, user: string}
end

Once suspended, ndrean will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, ndrean will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by ndrean will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If ndrean is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Thanks for keeping DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻 safe. Here is what you can do to flag ndrean:

ndrean consistently posts content that violates DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging ndrean will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: ndrean

item.title: React Router 6 延迟获取 | React Router 6 Deferred Fetch
item.description:
有时您希望检索一些可选数据而不阻塞包含应用程序关键数据的页面的其余部分。 此可选数据的示例包括在主要内容之后呈现的博客文章的评论、购物车上的推荐产品、最近的搜索等。

最好的部分是，您可以通过在解析数据的 Promise 中添加或删除 await 关键字来在一种模式或另一种模式之间切换。 我查看了React Router 示例文件夹和文档中的延迟演示应用程序，以发现它的所有潜力，但是，我找不到fetch的示例，所以我决定试一试，在这里是我发现的。

将defer与 Fetch 一起使用

我用 MSW 创建了一个模拟服务器来模拟提取延迟并显示示例中的相同文本。

这是我第一次天真的尝试：

 // Don't copy paste! bad code! keep on reading... export const loader = async () => { return defer ({ critical1 : await fetch ( ' /test?text=critical1&delay=250 ' ). then ( res => res . json ()), critical2 : await fetch ( ' /test?text=critical2&delay=500 ' ). then ( res => res . json ()), lazyResolved : await fetch ( ' /test?text=lazyResolved&delay=0 ' ). then ( res => res . json ()), lazy1 : fetch ( ' /test?text=lazy1&delay=1000 ' ). then ( res => res . json ()), lazy2 : fetch ( ' /test?text=lazy2&delay=1500 ' ). then ( res => res . json ()), lazy3 : fetch ( ' /test?text=lazy3&delay=2000 ' ). then ( res => res . json ()), lazyError : fetch ( ' /test?text=lazy3&delay=2500 ' ). then ( res => { throw Error ( ' Oh noo! ' ) }), }); }

那么我们在这里做什么呢？

首先，从普通加载器返回一个“裸提取”是可行的，因为这是加载器所期望的，React Router 会为你解包响应，但是， defer接受解析为值的值或承诺，所以要获取我们需要解包的值手动获取响应。

收货很棒！ 但是，您必须做一些额外的工作，包括像我们上面所做的那样抛出错误和解包承诺。 使用平台耶！ 结果如下：

一切看起来都很棒，直到我打开网络选项卡并且看起来不太对劲🤔

使用await关键字的前两个关键数据请求是在瀑布中发生的，而不是像其他请求那样并行！ 实际上，如果您将 await 添加到 defer 对象中的所有调用中，它们都发生在瀑布中，什么给了！

  • 瑞恩对我们撒谎了吗？

  • 这是一个错误吗？ 关键数据是否需要以瀑布形式出现？ 不！当然不是！ 原来我忘记了 Javascript 是如何工作的 ™️

    瀑布的发生是因为每次您创建一个单独的await时，它都会在继续下一行和触发下一个 fetch 之前暂停执行。

    为了避免瀑布，我们想要做的是同时触发所有这些 fetch 请求，并且只await响应而不是实际的fetch 。

    为了实现这一点，我们可以声明并触发所有获取请求，然后稍后在 defer 对象中添加关键数据的await 。

     // You can copy this one if you want export const loader = async () => { // fire them all at once const critical1Promise = fetch ( ' /test?text=critical1&delay=250 ' ). then ( res => res . json ()); const critical2Promise = fetch ( ' /test?text=critical2&delay=500 ' ). then ( res => res . json ()); const lazyResolvedPromise = fetch ( ' /test?text=lazyResolved&delay=100 ' ). then ( res => res . json ()); const lazy1Promise = fetch ( ' /test?text=lazy1&delay=500 ' ). then ( res => res . json ()); const lazy2Promise = fetch ( ' /test?text=lazy2&delay=1500 ' ). then ( res => res . json ()); const lazy3Promise = fetch ( ' /test?text=lazy3&delay=2500 ' ). then ( res => res . json ()); const lazyErrorPromise = fetch ( ' /test?text=lazy3&delay=3000 ' ). then ( res => { throw Error ( ' Oh noo! ' ) }); // await for the response return defer ({ critical1 : await critical1Promise , critical2 : await critical2Promise , lazyResolved : lazyResolvedPromise , lazy1 : lazy1Promise , lazy2 : lazy2Promise , lazy3 : lazy3Promise , lazyError : lazyErrorPromise }) }

    你也可以做Promise.all()但通过上面的例子，更容易理解发生了什么。

    这是网络选项卡现在的样子，漂亮的平行绿色条。

    现在我们修复了瀑布流，让我们来玩一下延迟并探索延迟的几个有趣的特性：

    关键数据

    关键数据使用 'await' 关键字，因此加载器和 React Router 将在第一次渲染之前等待数据准备好（没有加载微调器🎉）。

    如果关键数据（使用等待）返回错误会发生什么？ 如果你想优雅地失败并且不想破坏整个页面，那么删除 await 基本上告诉 React Router，嘿！ 我不在乎这个数据是否失败，它不是那么重要（关键），所以显示一个本地化的错误边界。 这正是第一个示例中的lazyError所做的。

    懒惰解决

    我们没有在lazyResolved字段上使用await ，但是我们根本看不到加载微调器。 那个怎么样？ 这实际上是 defer 的一个惊人功能，如果您的可选数据很快（比您的关键数据快），那么您根本不会看到微调器，因为您的承诺将在关键数据完成和第一次渲染发生时解决：

    最慢的关键数据延迟是500ms ，但lazyResolved数据只需要100ms ，所以当critical2被解析时， lazyResolved承诺已经被解析并且数据立即可用。

    您可以尝试更改延迟并增加/减少时间以查看是否显示微调器。 例如，如果我们将lazyResolved的延迟增加到3500ms ，我们将看到一个加载微调器。

    结论

    示例的源代码可在此处获得：

    https://github.com/infoxicator/react-router-defer-fetch https://github.com/infoxicator/react-router-defer-fetch



原文:

Sometimes you want to retrieve some optional data without blocking the rest of the page that contains the critical data of your application. Examples of this optional data are comments on a blog post that render after the main content, recommended products on a shopping cart, recent searches, etc.

React Router 6 introduced the "deferred" API that allows you to "await" for critical data and "defer" optional data when calling your loaders.

The best part is that you can switch between one mode or the other by just adding or removing the await keyword from the promise that resolves the data. Ryan Florence gave an excellent explanation of this mechanism in his talk "When to Fetch" (seriously, it is an amazing talk, If you haven't watched, bookmark it and watch it after you have finished reading this post!)

I took a look at the deferred demo app from the React Router examples folder and the documentation to discover all its potential, however, I couldn't find an example with fetch so I decided to give it a go and play around with it, here is what I found.

Using defer with Fetch

I created a mock server with MSW to simulate a fetch delay as well as display the same text from the example.

Here is my first naive attempt:

// Don't copy paste! bad code! keep on reading...
export const loader = async () => { return defer({ critical1: await fetch('/test?text=critical1&delay=250').then(res => res.json()), critical2: await fetch('/test?text=critical2&delay=500').then(res => res.json()), lazyResolved: await fetch('/test?text=lazyResolved&delay=0').then(res => res.json()), lazy1: fetch('/test?text=lazy1&delay=1000').then(res => res.json()), lazy2: fetch('/test?text=lazy2&delay=1500').then(res => res.json()), lazy3: fetch('/test?text=lazy3&delay=2000').then(res => res.json()), lazyError: fetch('/test?text=lazy3&delay=2500').then(res => { throw Error('Oh noo!') }), });
}

So what are we doing here?

First, returning a "naked fetch" from a normal loader works because that's what loaders expect and React Router will unwrap the response for you, however, defer accepts values or promises that resolve to values, so to get the values we need to unwrap the fetch response manually.

Fetch is great! however, you have to do some additional work, including throwing errors and unwrapping promises as we have done above. Use the platform yay! 😅

Here is the result:

It all looked great until I opened the network tab and something didn't look quite right 🤔

The first two requests for critical data that use the await keyword are happening in a waterfall, not in parallel like the rest! in fact, if you add await to all the calls in the defer object they all happen in a waterfall, what gives!

  • Did Ryan lie to us?

  • Is this a bug? 🐛

  • Does critical data need to happen in a waterfall? 🚿

Nope!, of course not! it turns out that I forgot how Javascript works™️

The waterfall occurs because every time you create a separate await, it will pause execution before continuing to the next line and before firing the next fetch.

What we want to do to avoid the waterfall is fire all those fetch requests at the same time and only await for the response not the actual fetch.

“The earlier you initiate a fetch, the better, because the sooner it starts, the sooner it can finish”

— @TkDodo

To achieve this we can declare and fire all the fetch requests and then add the await for critical data in the defer object later.


// You can copy this one if you want
export const loader = async () => { // fire them all at once  const critical1Promise = fetch('/test?text=critical1&delay=250').then(res => res.json()); const critical2Promise = fetch('/test?text=critical2&delay=500').then(res => res.json()); const lazyResolvedPromise = fetch('/test?text=lazyResolved&delay=100').then(res => res.json()); const lazy1Promise = fetch('/test?text=lazy1&delay=500').then(res => res.json()); const lazy2Promise = fetch('/test?text=lazy2&delay=1500').then(res => res.json()); const lazy3Promise = fetch('/test?text=lazy3&delay=2500').then(res => res.json()); const lazyErrorPromise = fetch('/test?text=lazy3&delay=3000').then(res => { throw Error('Oh noo!') }); // await for the response return defer({ critical1: await critical1Promise, critical2: await critical2Promise, lazyResolved: lazyResolvedPromise, lazy1: lazy1Promise, lazy2: lazy2Promise, lazy3: lazy3Promise, lazyError: lazyErrorPromise })
}

You can also do Promise.all() but with the above example, it is easier to understand what's going on.

Here is what the network tab looks like now, beautiful parallel green bars.

Now that we fixed the waterfall, let's play around with the delay and explore a couple of interesting features of defer:

Critical Data

The critical data uses the 'await' keyword so the loader and React Router will wait until the data is ready before the first render (no loading spinners 🎉).

What happens if critical data (using await) returns an error? 🤔 well, the loader will throw and bubble up to the nearest error boundary and destroy the entire page or that entire route segment.

If you want to fail gracefully and don't want to destroy the entire page then remove await which is basically telling React Router, hey! I don't care if this data fails, it is not that important (critical) so display a localised error boundary instead. That's exactly what the lazyError is doing in the first example.

Lazy Resolved

We are not using an await on the lazyResolved field, however we don't see a loading spinner at all. How is that? This is actually an amazing feature of defer, if your optional data is fast (faster than your critical data) then you won't see a spinner at all because your promise will be resolved by the time the critical data finishes and the first render occurs:

The slowest critical data delay is 500ms but the lazyResolved data takes only 100ms so by the time critical2is resolved, the lazyResolved promise has already been resolved and the data is immediately available.

The best thing about defer is that you don't have to choose how to fetch your data, it will display optional data immediately if it is fast or shows a loading spinner if it is slow.

You can play around changing the delays and increasing/reducing the time to see if the spinners are shown or not. For example, if we increase the delay for the lazyResolved to 3500ms we will see a loading spinner.

Conclusion

Defer is a great API, it took me a while to understand it and make it work with fetch but it is an amazing tool to improve performance, reliability of your pages and developer experience.

The Source code for the examples is available here:

https://github.com/infoxicator/react-router-defer-fetch
item.author: infoxicator

item.title: Cryptopals #46：想要一篇文章吗？ | Cryptopals #46: want a write-up?
item.description:
我想知道是否有人对https://cryptopals.com/sets/6/challenges/46的文章感兴趣？ 我正在用 Nodejs 完成它，看起来你可以很容易地找到挑战的代码，但没有实际的英文写作。

这主要是关于它是多么人为（他们警告过），所以我在 OpenSSL 中找不到真实的东西或充当受害者。

杰瑞德威尔科特 - 安东·雅库托维奇—— 一旦暂停，skaunov 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，skaunov 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发表，skaunov 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 skaunov 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 skaunov 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 skaunov 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

I wonder if anybody is interested in https://cryptopals.com/sets/6/challenges/46 write-up? I'm finishing it with Nodejs, and it seems like you can easily find code for the challenge, but no actual write-up in English.

It's mostly about how artificial it is (they warned) so I couldn't find a realistic something in OpenSSL or to act as victim.

The Jared Wilcurt - Sep 16

Anton Yakutovich - Oct 18

Once suspended, skaunov will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, skaunov will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by skaunov will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If skaunov is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Thanks for keeping DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻 safe. Here is what you can do to flag skaunov:

skaunov consistently posts content that violates DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging skaunov will restore default visibility to their posts.
